_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of file system implementations (e.g., New Technology File System [NTFS], File Allocation Table [FAT], File Extension [EXT]).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-020 (AI, cosine: 0.546), DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.422).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.7/10 (raw 0.473). Shared vocabulary: [file, knowledge, system]. DCWF-only terms [allocation, ext, extension, fat, implementations, new] vs WIDAI-only terms [command, common, data, engineering, fundamentals, line] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.846 (P=0.835, R=0.858). R exceeds P by 0.022 — the WIDAI KSA's 32 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 18 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (80.5%). "Knowledge of file system implementations (e.g., New Technology File System [NTFS" and "Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, i" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.566 > STS 0.473 (gap +0.092). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [file, knowledge, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.3%. Shared tokens: [file, knowledge, system]. DCWF-only: [allocation, ext, extension, fat, implementations, new]. WIDAI-only: [command, common, data, engineering, fundamentals, line].
